description: Quran display fonts bundled with the engine. Register the TTF, then render Arabic text with the matching postScriptName. See ../../docs/fonts.md for guidance on which to use.

fonts.label: Uthmani (Hafs)
fonts.credit: King Fahd Glorious Quran Printing Complex (KFGQPC)
fonts.notes: Crisp, well-separated letters — best for per-letter tajweed coloring. The default for the bundled Hafs text and the tajweed annotation corpus.

fonts.label: Qiraat (Qunbul Uthmani)
fonts.credit: King Fahd Glorious Quran Printing Complex (KFGQPC)
fonts.notes: Uthmani-style font that renders glyphs/marks present in the other qiraat (Warsh, Qaloon, Duri, Susi, Bazzi, Qunbul, Shubah) that the standard Uthmani.ttf does not support. Use it to display the data/qiraat/* readings. Trade-off: its marks tend to blend/overlap, so per-letter tajweed coloring is less clean than with Uthmani.ttf.

fonts.label: Indopak (Nastaliq)
fonts.credit: Ayman Siddiqui and R. Siddiqua (Al Mushaf / Urdu Nigar)
fonts.notes: The Indo-Pak / South-Asian Nastaliq writing style, familiar to readers in the Indian subcontinent. Different orthographic conventions from the Uthmani script; the bundled per-ayah UTF-16 tajweed offsets are computed against the Uthmani text, so coloring is intended for the Uthmani fonts.
